summary: A scorching '83 show highlighted by Phil Lesh’s turned‑up bass; recording source isn’t specified but fans rave about the performance.
review: Attendees describe this concert as one of the hottest nights of the 1983 tour, with Phil Lesh “turned all the way up” and the entire band on fire. The recordings are cherished (even a worn‑out cassette copy exists), though reviewers give no concrete details about the technical source or mix quality.

ai_rating.rationale: Two enthusiastic 5‑star reviews praise the show’s energy and Phil’s bass, while one negative comment offers no useful performance info; overall rating reflects a strong show with unknown recording quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No reviewer mentions soundboard, audience or matrix details; audio quality cannot be assessed from the comments.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans note an exceptionally hot performance with everyone “on fire,” especially Phil’s dominant bass presence throughout the night.

band_member_comments.Phil: Bass was turned all the way up from the start, dominating the mix.
